zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes De Samon於大約1479年出生在英國賀特福爾比曉普也斯 斯托特福德時，她的父親Sir Robert Samon20歲，母親Elizabeth Fiske25歲。她於1501年與Thomas Bisshop在英國英格蘭賀特福爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1564年1月4日在英國瓦維其可爾米爾福頓享年86歲，埋葬在英國瓦維其可爾米爾福頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes De Samon于大约1479年出生在英国賀特福爾比曉普也斯 斯托特福德时，她的父亲Sir Robert Samon20岁，母亲Elizabeth Fiske25岁。她于1501年与Thomas Bisshop在英国英格兰賀特福爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1564年1月4日在英国瓦維其可爾米爾福頓享年86岁，埋葬在英国瓦維其可爾米爾福頓。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Samon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire), French, Walloon, Breton, German, Dutch, Hungarian, northern Italian, and Jewish (Ashkenazic); Spanish (Simón); Czech and Slovak (mainly Šimon); Slovenian, Croatian, and Rusyn (from Slovakia) (also Šimon): from the Biblical personal name, Hebrew Shim‘on, which is probably derived from the Hebrew verb sham‘a ‘to hearken’. In the Vulgate and in many vernacular versions of the Old Testament, this is usually rendered Simeon . In the Greek New Testament, however, the name occurs as Simōn, as a result of assimilation to the pre-existing Greek byname Sīmōn (from sīmos ‘snub-nosed’). Both Simon and Simeon were in use as personal names in western Europe from the Middle Ages onward. In Christendom the former was always more popular, at least in part because of its associations with the apostle Simon Peter, the brother of Andrew. In Britain there was also confusion from an early date with Anglo-Scandinavian forms of Sigmund(r) or Sigmund (see Siegmund ), a name whose popularity was reinforced at the Conquest by the Norman form Simund. In North America, this surname has also absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Italian Simone , Polish Szymon, Albanian Simoni , and Assyrian/Chaldean or Arabic Shimun, Shamon , or Shamoun , and also their derivatives (see examples at Simons ). See also Shimon .

History: André Simon dit Boucher from France married Marie Martin in Acadia c. 1688. François Simon from Saint-Pair-sur-Mer in Manche, France, married Marie-Dorothée Gagnon in Rivière-Ouelle, QC, in 1744.

variants.name: Simeon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Gabrielle, Jean-Claude, Laurent, Laurette, Marcel, Marie Carmel, Marielle, Michel, Micheline, Reynald, Serge, Wilner.  Spanish Eduardo, Ricardo, Roberto, Aida, Alfredo, Efren, Francisco, Guillermo, Juan Cruz, Mario, Maximo, Miguel.

French, English, Croatian, and West Indian (mainly Haiti); Spanish (Simeón): from the French, Middle English, and Croatian personal name Simeon, Spanish Simeón, in origin the Biblical Hebrew name Shim‘on, also rendered Simon . The name Simeon was chosen in honor of any of many Christian saints so named, including Saint Simeon Stylites, an ascetic 5th-century Syrian monk who lived for 40 years on top of a pillar. As a surname of Spanish origin it is most common in the Philippines.
American shortened form of Greek patronymics from the personal name Simeon (see 1 above), such as Simeonidis or Simeonoglou.

variants.name: Mona
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Mohammed, Wedad, Ghalib, Jamal, Khadijah, Muna, Showkat.
Italian: from the feminine form of Mone, a short form of the personal name Simone (equivalent of Simon ).
Muslim: from a personal name based on Arabic munā ‘wishes, desires’, plural of munya ‘wish’.

variants.name: McKim
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Shim, a patronymic from a Gaelic pet form of the personal name Simon . Compare McKimmy .

variants.name: McKimmy
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Shimidh, ‘son of Simidh’, a Gaelic pet form of Simon . Compare McKim .

variants.name: Monetti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Alessandro, Luciano, Domenico, Gabriele, Gino, Giuliano, Raffaele, Sal, Salvatore.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of Monetto, a shortened petform of the personal name Simone (see Simon ).

variants.name: McKimmie
meanings: Scottish (northeastern, especially Banffshire and Morayshire): from Gaelic Mac Shimidh ‘son of Simidh’, a Gaelic pet form of the personal name Simon . Compare McKimm and McSkimming .

variants.name: Monet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philippe, Pierre.  Spanish Domingo, Carlos, Cesar.
French: from a shortened pet form of Simon or another personal name ending in -mon, such as Aymon (see Aymond ). This surname is also found in Cuba. Compare Monette .
Altered form of French   Moinet  .

variants.name: Monell
meanings: Probably an altered form of French Monel: from a shortened pet form of Simon or another personal name ending in -mon, such as Aymon (see Aymond ). This surname was brought to North America from Ireland.

variants.name: McKimm
meanings: Irish and Scottish: from Mac Shim, a patronymic from a Gaelic pet form of Simon . Compare McKimmie .

variants.name: Moen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Selmer, Egil, Alf, Anders, Ardeen, Hilmer, Juel, Knut, Nels, Obert, Orvald.
Dutch: from a short form of the personal name Simo(e)n (see Simon ).
In some cases possibly also an American shortened form of Flemish Van Moen: habitational name for someone from Moen in West Flanders.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes De Samon was born about 1479, in Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, England, her father, Sir Robert Samon, was 20 and her mother, Elizabeth Fiske, was 25. She married Thomas Bisshop in 1501, in Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 5 daughters. She died on 4 January 1564, in Milverton, Warwickshire, England, at the age of 86, and was buried in Milverton, Warwickshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes De Samon est née environ 1479, à Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Robert Samon, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Fiske, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Bisshop en 1501, à Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 4 janvier 1564, à Milverton, Warwickshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 86 ans, et a été enterrée à Milverton, Warwickshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes De Samon um 1479 in Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Robert Samon 20 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Fiske 25. Sie hat 1501 in Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom Thomas Bisshop geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 4. Januar 1564 im Alter von 86 Jahren in Milverton, Warwickshire, England und wurde in Milverton, Warwickshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes De Samon è nata il circa 1479 nel Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, England, sua padre, Sir Robert Samon, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Fiske, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Bisshop nel 1501, nel Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 5 figlie. Morì il 4 gennaio 1564, nel Milverton, Warwickshire, England, all'età di 86 anni, e fu sepolta a Milverton, Warwickshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes De Samonが約1479年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアビショプ'ス ストルトフォードで生まれた時，父Sir Robert Samonは20歳，母Elizabeth Fiskeは25歳であった。1501年にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアでThomas Bisshopと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1564年1月4日にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアミルバートンで86歳で死去し，イギリスワーウィクシュアイアミルバートンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes De Samon(이)가 대략 1479년 영국 허트퍼드실르 비숍'스 스토르트퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert Samon(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Fiske(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1501년 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르에서 Thomas Bisshop(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1564년 1월 4일 영국 워윅실르 미버턴에서 86세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워윅실르 미버턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes De Samon nasceu aproximadamente 1479, em Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Robert Samon, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Fiske, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Bisshop em 1501, em Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 4 de janeiro de 1564, em Milverton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, com 86 anos, e foi sepultada em Milverton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes De Samon родилась около 1479 г. в местности бишоп'с стортфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Robert Samon было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Fiske, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Bisshop в 1501 г. в местности хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 4 января 1564 г. в местности милвиртон, варвиккшир, Англия в возрасте 86 лет и была похоронена в местности милвиртон, варвиккшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes De Samon nació aproximadamente 1479, en Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Robert Samon, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Fiske, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Thomas Bisshop en 1501, en Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 5 hijas. Murió el 4 de enero de 1564, en Milverton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 86 años, y fue sepultada en Milverton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra.
